description_long: This structure is a large, pole-mounted solar energy installation featuring a central cylindrical tower that supports a wide, circular solar panel array at the top. The base of the tower is surrounded by a circular platform with four extended walkways, each equipped with railings for safety. The central column appears to be constructed from metal, with visible seams and access points, possibly for maintenance or housing electrical components. The upper section includes a fenced observation or maintenance deck just below the solar panel array. The solar panels themselves are arranged in a broad, flat disc, optimized for maximum sunlight capture. The overall design suggests a robust, permanent installation suitable for urban parks or public spaces, providing both energy generation and potential public interaction.
description: A tall, pole-mounted solar energy structure with a wide circular solar panel array, surrounded by a platform and four walkways with railings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the central tower, circular platform, and four symmetrical walkways radiating outward.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting an attached box-like structure and a staircase leading up to the platform.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the central column, walkways, and some visible internal components or access panels.
description_view_3: Angled view showing both the staircase and the box-like structure, as well as the arrangement of the walkways.
attribute_mounting_structure_type: pole-mounted with steel and aluminum support structure
attribute_array_configuration: single, large circular array of solar panels forming a disc at the top
